prompt:
Boot the knowledge-graph v0 convention and use it to capture, at FULL fidelity, the ideas from closed PR #1 that were deliberately compressed during re-landing (PR #5). The owner re-read PR #1 and wants the complete thinking preserved — the layered docs keep their thin integrated versions; the full prose becomes idea nodes.

## Context — read first
- design/user-scenarios.md § S1 'Knowledge v0' — the convention this PR instantiates: markdown files with YAML frontmatter (type, id, status, title; edges later), no tooling, schemas are documentation.
- design/architecture.md — the thin outcome-DAG bet and federation paragraph (these STAY as-is; the nodes carry the fuller thinking).
- Closed PR #1 in full: `gh pr view 1 --json headRefName` then read design/outcomes.md, design/federation.md, design/self-transparency.md, and the README.md/knowledge.md/openquestions.md diffs from that branch (`gh api 'repos/gunk-dev/the-valley/contents/<path>?ref=<branch>'`). This prose is owner-authored; preserve its substance faithfully.

## What to create

**1. `.the-valley/README.md`** — the v0 convention, short: one file per node; frontmatter fields (type, id, status, title, created; optional source); type prefixes and their directories — `outcomes/oc-*`, `bugs/bd-*`, `ideas/ida-*`, `decisions/dcr-*`; status enums per type (e.g. idea: raw|exploring|adopted|superseded; outcome: open|in-progress|done|abandoned; bug: open|closed); IDs short hash-derived; creating/closing a node is a commit; ls/grep/git-log are the interface. Schemas are documentation, not enforcement — say so. Keep it to a page.

**2. `.the-valley/ideas/` — the PR #1 capture, full substance:**
- `ida-<hash>-outcome-dag.md` — the COMPLETE thinking from PR #1's design/outcomes.md: the generative reading of the dependency DAG, root outcomes and priority propagation, frontier dispatch on the critical path, klaus-shaped dispatch, how `closes` + attestation-success finalizes a node, the task→outcome rename rationale, the split of the priority-layer question into work-scheduling vs attention-routing. Frontmatter status: adopted (its thin form is an architecture bet); body notes 'thin form: architecture.md § the outcome DAG; this node carries the full sketch.'
- `ida-<hash>-federation-groups.md` — the COMPLETE thinking from PR #1's design/federation.md: group as unit of federation, one instance per group (bus + integrator + hosting), single-machine instantiable / distributable later, intra- vs inter-group split of the cross-repo questions, trust scoped within a group, discovery as a federation concern. Status: adopted (thin form: architecture.md § federation).
- If the PR #1 diffs to README.md or knowledge.md contain conceptual content NOT already present in main or in the two nodes above, capture the remainder in the most fitting node (or a third `ida-` node if it is genuinely distinct). Be systematic: after writing the nodes, re-walk every PR #1 hunk and confirm each idea now exists somewhere on main or in a node — list the mapping in the PR description, and explicitly name anything you judged already-fully-captured.
- design/self-transparency.md already exists on main as the stub — compare against PR #1's original; if the original had substance the stub dropped, append it to the stub's body (it is already marked DRAFT) rather than creating a node.

**3. `.the-valley/decisions/dcr-<hash>-pr1-relanding.md`** — a short decision node recording the disposition: PR #1 was unmergeable post-restructure; ideas re-landed thin at altitude (PR #5) with full prose preserved as idea nodes (this PR); outcomes.md/federation.md return as design docs when rungs S3+/S5+ are top-priority. Status: decided.

**4. Cross-links, one line each:** in design/architecture.md, at the end of the outcome-DAG section and the federation paragraph, add 'The fuller sketch: [.the-valley/ideas/ida-...](../.the-valley/ideas/ida-....md).' In design/user-scenarios.md § Knowledge v0, note the convention is now instantiated at [.the-valley/](../.the-valley/README.md). Touch nothing else in the layered docs.

## Constraints
- Fidelity over polish for the node bodies — adapt PR #1's prose minimally so each node reads standalone (source noted in frontmatter as `source: PR #1`), do not summarize it thinner.
- All relative links resolve (mind the ../ from design/ to .the-valley/).
- PR description: why (owner wants full ideas preserved; compression lost insight), the hunk-by-hunk PR #1 → destination mapping, and note this instantiates S1's knowledge-v0 convention with real content. No AI/assistant attribution, no Co-Authored-By.

Open a PR.
